summary: CC-0074 removes the hash-triggered Deployment rollout on fernet/credential key rotation. The keysHash, fernetKeysHash, and credentialKeysHash functions are deleted from reconcile_deployment.go, the buildKeystoneDeployment signature is reduced to 2 parameters, hash annotations are removed from the pod template, and volume mounts remain intact. 12 hash-related tests are replaced with 4 new tests (NoHashAnnotations, StablePodTemplate, VolumesMaintained, NoSecretReadRequired). E2E tests are updated to assert pod UID stability after rotation (no rollout) and fernet token validation across the rotation boundary. Documentation in keystone-reconciler.md, keystone-e2e-tests.md, and quick-start.md accurately reflects the new in-place rotation behavior.

tests_checklist.item: 12 hash-related tests removed (Fernet/Credential Hash Annotation, Deterministic, SecretNotFound, HashFromSecret)
New TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_NoHashAnnotations asserts absence of hash annotations
New TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_VolumesMaintained asserts 3 volumes and 3 mount paths unchanged
New TestReconcileDeployment_NoSecretReadRequired uses interceptor to prove no Secret reads
5 non-hash buildKeystoneDeployment call sites updated to 2-arg signature
E2E fernet-rotation Step 4 asserts pod UID stability and pre-rotation token validation
E2E credential-rotation Step 4 asserts pod UID stability
E2E scripts use set -euo pipefail with explicit error checks

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing Gomega test patterns (NewGomegaWithT, dot import)
Follows Chainsaw E2E test structure (SPDX header, step comments, try/catch)
Unused imports removed from both .go files (crypto/sha256, encoding/hex, encoding/json, apierrors, types)
No dead code remains — keysHash, fernetKeysHash, credentialKeysHash fully deleted
Code comment explains CC-0074 rationale on reconcileDeployment
golangci-lint clean on changed files (1 pre-existing issue in unchanged reconcile_networkpolicy_test.go)

security_checklist.item: No secrets hardcoded — E2E reads admin password from Kubernetes Secret
Volume mounts for fernet-keys and credential-keys remain read-only
No new API boundaries or user inputs introduced
Restricted security context on containers unchanged

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is pure removal — simplest possible approach
Relies on kubelet Secret projection (built-in K8s mechanism)
No new abstractions or indirection introduced
Documentation accurately describes in-place rotation flow

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Removed code was the only consumer of deleted imports

fail_fast_checklist.item: E2E scripts exit immediately on kubectl failures (set -euo pipefail)
TOKEN emptiness checked before proceeding to rotation
Secret data change verified before UID comparison

defensive_checklist.item: NoHashAnnotations test handles nil annotations map defensively
NoSecretReadRequired test uses interceptor for behavioral verification
E2E catch blocks collect diagnostic output on failure

suggested_improvements: The .planwerk/patterns/sub-reconciler-condition-contract-with-observedgeneration.md pattern file references the now-removed keysHash/fernetKeysHash/credentialKeysHash shared helper pattern. Consider updating the pattern description in a follow-up to remove the stale paragraph about hash computation.

E2E fernet-rotation test: the TOKEN capture interpolates ADMIN_PASS into curl arguments visible in process listing. Acceptable in E2E test context (SUP5) but could use stdin-based approach for consistency with the 'never pass secrets as CLI arguments' review pattern if E2E security posture is tightened in the future.

TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_NoHashAnnotations: since the Annotations field is now removed entirely, a direct g.Expect(annotations).To(BeNil()) assertion would be slightly more explicit than the if-nil guard, though current approach is functionally correct.
next_steps: Update .planwerk/patterns/sub-reconciler-condition-contract-with-observedgeneration.md to remove stale hash helper paragraph in a follow-up
